results.title: Gas in portal circulation and pneumatosis cystoides intestinalis during chemotherapy for advanced rectal cancer.
results.abstract: Acute abdominal symptoms with CT scan evidence of intramural gas in bowel walls (pneumatosis cystoides intestinalis, PCI) and of gas in the portal venous blood (PBG) in patients undergoing chemotherapy may represent a worrisome picture, suggestive of bowel necrosis. This picture remains a major clinical clue and the reporting of new cases may help to share awareness and experience on management. We describe a patient with acute abdominal symptoms and evidence of PCI with PBG under cetuximab, oxaliplatin, tegafur-uracil and folinic acid chemotherapy for metastatic adenocarcinoma of the rectosigmoid junction.

results.title: Determination of operation time in colorectal diseases: preoperative chemotherapy application.
results.abstract: Our aim was to determine the time it takes for wound healing to return to normal in cases where patients have undergone preoperative chemotherapy.
